channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Violent protests erupt in Chile over government cuts of social programmes
item.description: Labour unions and student groups protested in Chile as President Kast delivered his first State of the Nation.

item.title: US court upholds injunction against Trump policy banning transgender troops
item.description: The decision was split, allowing the Trump administration to continue barring transgender people from enlisting.

item.title: Who attended this year’s Israel Day Parade in New York?
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Ava Warriner takes a look at the Israeli and US officials who joined the annual Israel Day parade.

item.title: Video: Presidential election has Colombia on edge
item.description: Abelardo de la Espriella will face left-wing Senator Ivan Cepeda in the run-off for Colombia’s presidential election.

item.title: Foreign Christians ask for forgiveness over failure to support Israel at Knesset
item.description: ‘Lord, please forgive us for all the things that we did that we did not support Israel.’

item.title: Denmark gets new government as Greenland crisis persists
item.description: End of election deadlock will see PM Frederiksen continue her stewardship of response to Trump's Greenland lust.

item.title: Kenyans protest Ebola quarantine centre for US citizens
item.description: Hundreds have protested in central Kenya against a US-funded plan to quarantine Americans exposed to Ebola there.

item.title: Is the US-backed Gaza peace process in serious danger?
item.description: Israel killing more Palestinians, moves to seize 70 percent of Gaza Strip

item.title: Protesters call on Kenyan government to halt femicide crisis
item.description: Advocacy groups have given the government 40 days to intervene to deal with the plague of gender-based violence.

item.title: Armenian PM rejects Russia’s demand for EU referendum as relations nosedive
item.description: Russia is raising the pressure on Armenia as traditional ally looks to the West.

item.title: Trump halts $1.8bn ‘anti-weaponisation’ fund amid bipartisan backlash
item.description: The announcement comes after Trump met with congressional Republicans over concerns about his settlement with the IRS.

item.title: Former election clerk Tina Peters released after Trump pressure campaign
item.description: Peters had been sentenced to nine years in state prison after allowing access to Colorado state voting machines.

item.title: Gaza-bound aid ship begins voyage from Sweden
item.description: A Gaza-bound ship carrying aid began its voyage from Sweden, weeks after Israeli forces intercepted a previous aid ship.

item.title: Kenyans protest planned US Ebola quarantine facility
item.description: Hundreds protest near the site of the planned facility, which would serve US nationals exposed to the virus.

item.title: AI giant Anthropic files for US IPO as investors bet big on AI future
item.description: Anthropic, which operates AI chatbot Claude, did not disclose the size or the terms of the offering.

item.title: Israeli strike damages hospital in southern Lebanon
item.description: An Israeli attack near Jabal Amel Hospital in Tyre has destroyed wards and medical equipment.

item.title: Ethiopians head to polls as millions excluded from elections
item.description: Ethiopians head to polls as millions excluded from elections.

item.title: Activists plaster missing posters around Mexico stadiums ahead of World Cup
item.description: Activists put up missing persons posters to raise awareness of the disappearance crisis ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Former general for Syria’s Assad pleads not guilty in torture trial
item.description: Brigadier General Khaled al-Halabi faces charges of aggravated torture, coercion, sexual coercion and bodily harm.

item.title: Nvidia unveils new chip to bring AI directly to personal computers
item.description: Nvidia's new chip will power new Windows laptop and desktop computer models to make 'AI personal computers'.

item.title: Can cohosts Canada reach the knockout stages at World Cup 2026?
item.description: Canada are playing only their third World Cup, but have high hopes for a talented squad under coach Jesse Marsch.

item.title: International court rejects Rwanda’s claim over UK migration deal
item.description: Rwanda's $134m claim has been thrown out, which bodes ill for other governments seeking 'return hubs'.

item.title: Protest outside Kenya’s planned US Ebola quarantine centre
item.description: Hundreds rally against plan to send US nationals exposed to the virus to Laikipia Air Base.

item.title: Lebanon’s social grocery store fighting rising costs and displacement
item.description: ‘Man wa Salwa’ is a community-run social grocery store helping hundreds of families access affordable essentials.

item.title: Who will replace Newsom? What to know about California’s primary elections
item.description: The most populous US state has attracted attention for its contentious gubernatorial race with 61 candidates.
